rationale.R1.2: Delegation is explicitly and exclusively 'check the logical consistency.' This is load-bearing: the artifact contains at least two serious internal contradictions that must be surfaced. (1) The 'Deployment scope' section claims SUMSCREEN 'requires no self-report labels,' yet the entire training and evaluation pipeline is built on self-reported ground-truth labels — the model cannot exist without them. (2) The results section performs an invalid inferential leap: it derives the prevalence estimate (~41%) directly from the dataset's own self-report rate, then attributes that figure to the AUC of 0.93 ('From this AUC we therefore conclude…'), conflating classifier performance with a population-prevalence inference. AUC measures discrimination, not calibrated prevalence; the 41% figure is tautologically recovered from the training-set label distribution, not inferred from detector output. These are premises-to-conclusion coherence failures and cross-section contradictions squarely in R1.2 scope.
